update.body: Himalayas Update: our travelers have reached their camp - what a travel adventure!   ⛰🐏🏔

Hello families of our Foothills of the Himalayas trippers!
The group has landed and is on the way to their accommodations.  Here's to a wonderful week of adventure ahead!  ⛰🐏🏔

Dear Parents,
 We hope you are doing well. As we prepare for the upcoming Interim trip to India, we kindly remind all families to read through the Travel Guide for India.
 For this trip, all students must hold a valid passport and obtain the correct tourist visa for entry into India. Please note the following important information:


Hong Kong SAR and Chinese passport holders are not eligible for the India e‑Visa and must apply for a paper visa in person, at the Consulate General of India in Hong Kong.  


Processing time for residents is normally 3-5 working days, but in some cases, the Consulate may require additional verification, which can extend processing to 5-8 working days.




To avoid any delays or issues close to departure, we strongly encourage all HKSAR and Chinese passport holders to begin their visa application as early as possible. 


  If your child holds a passport from another nationality, please inform us and update this information in PowerSchool so we can ensure the correct visa requirements are communicated to you. 


 If you are eligible for an e-Visa, we recommend that parents apply for it around February 20th to avail themselves of a 30-day validity period, with the expiry date approximately March 19th. It is not recommended to apply before Feb 20 to avoid having to apply again. 


Important note about appointment booking
Paper visa applications require an advance appointment at the Consulate. At this time, the earliest available appointment is March 2. Time slots may open up as the Consulate updates its system daily, so we encourage you to keep checking the appointment website regularly and secure the earliest time that is convenient for your family.
In the meantime, HKIS and our program partner, Snow Leopard Adventures, are preparing all required documents (including school letters, flight itinerary, and supporting materials) and will share them with you shortly to support your child’s visa application.
All required documentation and application details are outlined in the Interim Travel Guide. You may also refer to the official visa site: https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance.
Warm regards,
HS Interim Office

assignment.description:
Submit the PDF copy of your India visa here as soon as you receive it. To download this you must click on the link inside the email you receive when it is granted.
If you are an Indian national then submit your PIO/OCI Card No, place of issue, date of issue and date valid until.
Bring a printed copy of your visa with you to the airport.
Your eVisa should look like this:

grade.comment: It's good that you'll have this record of events to look back on as you relive some of these moments.  You were a strong, positive force in this group Chloe, and we appreciated your care in connecting people and living in the moment. It would be good to consider how you can create those connections in new groups. Best wishes for these last few months.

Gabi, thank you for this reflection and it's clear that you grew in this week in building relationships within the group as well as in the school. I think you are realizing the power of social connections and growing together. Your activities in the school fostered a sense of fun, laughter and play, while giving the students opportunities to learn and grow. You have a talent here and we're looking forward to your updates of your next chapters.  Cheers!
Thanks for sharing Kira.  It's clear that you valued the sense of community in this trip. it would be worth thinking about how you can recreate that with future teams/groups that you are a part of.

Neel, what you wrote was very succinct and I believe you could explore more of the week in your reflection.  It's clear that you grew in your relationships with everyone in the group and it's clear that you became comfortable with everyone.  I think that is what you mean by "it's worth it". Thank you for your positive energy and friendliness to all in the group. You helped make this team a great group.

Maia, thanks for sharing this succinct reflection. It's clear that you appreciated the relationships that grew over the week. Are there any specific activities or events that helped spark this development? It's worth thinking about this as you consider how to grow future groups to feel as deeply and inclusive. Thoughts of a leader....Thanks for being a positive and inclusive participant on this trip.

Thanks for sharing this Athena. It sounds like you appreciated being stretched outside your comfort zone, which may have created a higher energy state in developing those deeper friendships. Having a common challenge.  Thanks for your positive energy through the trip.

Thank you for the thoughtful reflection Lilliana. It's clear that you appreciated so much on this interim, with the relationships being your priority. We appreciate your feedback about the group size and recognize this as well. It will be also good for you to consider how you as a student can help to create this bonding experience in future trips.

Daniel, thanks for this reflection. It's clear that you appreciated so much in this trip, and although you mention friends, it is worth noting how your positive, helpful and friendly nature elevated the whole group. You have a way about you that makes others feel comfortable and welcome, and you may want to consider this as you think of your leadership style and bringing out the best in a group. Keep up that positive, inclusive style in future trips.

Thank you for sharing this reflection Stuart. It's clear that you put some thought into this, and we're glad that you made meaningful connections. It makes us realize that we need more airport games at the front end to start the process even sooner.  Thank you for your positive and supportive nature through all the activities. You have a good way of bringing up the group and finding the fun.

Hi Jerry - seems like the rafting and cold water was a big memory for you (btw it was probably 17oC). You developed resilience in knowing you can overcome hard times and be okay. You make a good point about showing respect as a doorway to getting to know people - it's great that you developed some new and positive relationships on this trip. Asking questions and being curious can add to this development.  Good luck in the rest of your senior year as you prepare for this next chapter.
Ryan,  A reflection usually goes deeper than this, and it would be good for you to articulate more about the activities in the interim and moments that may have inspired you or helped you to grow.

Thanks for sharing Annabelle. It sounds like you discovered some perspectives for life - from being adventurous with food and activities to creating new and special relationships through those activities.  It would be good to think about how you can be a force for creating these relationships on your next interim trip - and beyond.

Chloe, thank you for sharing your thoughts in this reflection. It's clear that this trip was deeply meaningful to you and the key is in the relationships.  I'm so glad that you were able to connect and also appreciate the bigger connections with our unique environment. It's worth exploring how you can recreate that in future teams and groups so that everyone feels included. Thank you for your positive energy and support in this trip.

Kyle, It's good that you'll have this record of events to look back on as you relive some of these moments.  You were a positive force in this group and we appreciated your sense of fun. It would be good to consider how you can create community and connections in new groups in future interims.

Thank you for sharing in this thoughtful and percepective reflection Elena. I enjoyed reading this and am thankful that you were able to consider your growth in a positive and unique way. It is an interesting angle to think of boredom as a path to creativity.  And your effort in your gift of your drawing  was appreciated by the owner, Ajeet Bajaj who wrote to personally thank you.  I hope your future trip are as thought provoking and creative.

Thanks for sharing this reflection Kavya. It sounds like you were pushed outside of your comfort zone in this week and it has made you reflect on the power of doing that. The reflection of your accomplishments has created a new confidence in yourself and it's great that you recognize this. I'm also curious about the games you mentioned - please share those in a message if you have time.  I know next year you'll take charge and offer up games to the new group, knowing what it takes to build connections and elevate the group.  Proud of you for daring to take risks.

Shreya, thank you for sharing this reflection. It seems that you appreciated many aspects that helped you understand your culture, while giving you a sense of how it will be when you are in a new environment at college with many people you don't know.  Your steady, positive nature reveals itself and people gravitate to you. It was a pleasure having you on this trip.

update.body:
Welcome to 2025-2026
Students, welcome to the first day of school.  Given the RED RAIN, we are switching to online learning for today.  Classes will be entirely online - please follow the following schedule and use the link to meet at 8:45 for an information session for all.
 





THURSDAY, AUGUST 14 
Day 7(B)- Special Schedule – ONLINE 




8:45 – 9:00 


Welcome Back Message & Information (All) 
Use this link- https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NGFkNjZiNGEtZDkyMC00NTNlLWI0NjgtOWE3YTMxZjgxZDg5%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2251de2916-601d-4341-8a59-c7cb27133ac2%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%22dea9ab52-0969-438a-bb72-306044fa2731%22%7d




9:20 – 9:40 


Check in with your PCG 




9:50 – 10:10 


Block 7 




10:20 – 10:40 


Block 1 




10:45 – 11:05 


Block 3 




11:10-11:30 


Block 5 
 




11:35 – 11:50 


PCG Teachers available for check in, information

Graduation Seating Plan
Happy rainy graduation day to all!
⁠This is a copy of the graduation seating plan for this evening (scroll down to page 2). When you come up from the 100's you will be in two lines- the line on the right head to the dedicated HS Faculty seating on the right, faculty on the left, head left.
As a reminder of timings (all approximate):
4pm: Snacks available in the 100's
4:15-4:45: Gowning and random selfies
4:45: Start lining up
4:55: Move out of the 100's
5pm: Process into the gym

This message went to students today: 
Semester Assessment Schedule
Hi Students.  We are in the homestretch with only 1 more week and 1 day of regular classes left before special schedule week. Please refer to the Special Schedule linked to this post.  It is your responsibility to ensure you know what assessments or reassessments you have, when you have them, and where these will take place.  Please follow up with your teacher if you are unsure.

This messgae went to Seniors today: 
Missing Assignments
I have been receiving reports from some teachers that there are still incomplete assignments in some courses.  If you do not submit your missing learning today by 4 pm the following will happen: 
- You will receive an IE in Schoology for the assignment.  Schoology treats an IE as a zero. 
- You will be required to come to school on Wednesday to complete the assignment. 
- Your report card will not be issued on time, and as a result, we will not be able to submit your final transcript to your university on time
The BEST solution: complete all your learning and submit it by 4pm today!
Thank you for ending well.

Senior Project Thanks and Early Release Day 'To Do.'
Many, many, many thanks for your support, encouragement and flexibility as we completed Senior Project Day yesterday. Initial feedback from the students provides some really constructive suggestions on how we can continue to improve that experience and we welcome your feedback too, using the form.
Our afternoon schedule for ERD is:
11:30- 12:30- LUNCH
12:30- 1:15- Senior Project presentation 'make ups' (but I think we cleared them all yesterday so please use the time constructively for assessment and planning; we'll let you know if we need you)
1:15- 2pm- 504/ SEL: where have we been and where are we going?
2:15- 3:45pm- 504/ Celebrating Engaging Strategies w/ Ellen Mahoney. The agenda is attached.
As a reminder, please come to the afternoon session ready to talk and share based on the following prompt:

TA Openings
This is a friendly reminder that you have until May 23 to hand in your TA application to the sponsoring teacher. NO LATE APPLICATIONS WILL BE ACCEPTED.  Be sure to talk to your counselor about whether you are actually available for the block the class runs before applying.

TA Openings
One more opening came available.  You can check the complete list of TA openings below.

This message went out to students today: 
TA and Performing Arts Leadership Applications Now Open
TA: If you are interested in a TA position, please see the openings for 25-26.  If there is an opening you are interested in, please see your counselor to ensure that the block is available in your schedule. If the block is available, please fill in the application form found in the Academic Handbook and give it to the sponsoring TA teacher.  Applications are due by May 23.  LATE APPLICATIONS WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED!
Performing Arts Leadership: Fill in the application form found in the Academic Handbook and give it to your PA teacher.  Applications are due June 6.  
Good luck!

Reminder: Secondary School Update at 3:15 in the AUD
Just a reminder that Ben and Ron will join us at 3:15 to share information about the Secondary School model. If you are available at that time, please join us.

Good morning all, 
The AP Art Exhibition runs April 8 - May 1 with a reception evening on Thursday, April 24th. We’re in block B this year with units on several floors (see the poster below for details). Hope some of you can make it either for the reception or just to pop in. Students have been working very hard and we’re super excited to see it finally come together!

Student Feedback Surveys
If you forgot to give students time to complete the TRIPOD surveys last week, you have today and tomorrow.  Thank you for giving the time.

TRIPOD: DAY 2
Good morning.  Thank you for giving 10 minutes in EACH class for students to complete the TRIPOD.  
 These courses are NOT surveyed: 

Seminar 
Learning Strategies (too few students so hard to keep anonymous) 
Performing Arts Student Leadership 
GOA 
Teaching Assistant 
Elementary Teaching Assistant 
Structured Study 
History 9 and History 10 
Internship 
Independent Study 
Senior Option 
PCG 
Senior Project Process 
Senior Project Presentation 
HK National Sport

Lost and Found Tables Outside HS Main Office
 
Teachers and staff, please bring any unclaimed items from your classrooms and offices to me.
Tomorrow students will be checking for their lost items outside the main office or with me (I hope).

Do you need a web app? 
Our Advanced Computer Science Studio students are getting ready to build it for you! Partner with us as a client and guide your very own talented team in creating a custom web app prototype that meets your needs. Places are limited so click the link to sign up today! 🚀

M365 Support Session - Thursday, 23rd Jan @ 3:15pm (in HS405L)

Just a reminder that the IT Department is offering a drop-in session today (Thursday, 23/1/2025) to support the transition to Microsoft. The session will run in HS405L (HS Library) from 3:15-4:00pm.

Good morning all, 
The schedule for today went in student life but I will cross post it here and in Teams for those who want it.
The schedule is as follows:
8:30- first bell
8:35- 9:45- period 1/ block 5
9:55- 11:05- period 2/ block 7
11:05- 11:45- LUNCH
11:50- 1pm- period 3/ block 1
1:10- 2:20pm- period 4/ block 3
2:25- 3pm- Interim Meeting 1 (Check your rooms here)

ALUMNI EVENT:  Tuesday, January 7, 10:30 am - 12 pm 
H504
Advancement will be hosting the Class of 2024 in Room 504 tomorrow from 10:30-12 pm. for a Meet and Greet.  Teachers are invited to drop by to say hello. Alums are also invited to stay for lunch if they like in the Cafeteria. Drinks and pastries are being provided by the Alumni Office and Karena Belin, Board of Directors and mom of Celeste Belin, Class of 2024.
If you have a moment, please consider coming by to say hello and catch up with our friends from the Class of 2024!

ALUMNI EVENT:  THE FAERIE QUEENE

'An outcast. A golden boy. A girl trying to figure things out. An act of malice and a fickle game of cat and mouse. In an uncanny character study about the toxicity and disarray of modern teen culture, three young souls are driven to the edge over questions that have no answers.'  



THE FAERIE QUEENE, was written, produced, and directed by Lenny Luo (22') during his senior year, with the involvement and support of many HKIS students, alumni, and community members. The film had its world premiere this previous summer at the 27th Brooklyn Film Festival in NYC, where it was one of 12 selected features from around the world. On Tuesday, January 7th, at 6pm, HKIS will be hosting a friends and family screening of the film in the HS auditorium. The director and some of the alumni involved in the project will be present for a Q&A after the film! 
Note:  this film addresses difficult and mature themes. It includes profanity, violence, and implied sexual content. It is recommended that parents accompany audience members under 17. If you have any questions about this, feel free to message the directior, Lenny Luo at lennyluo69@gmail.com
If you are interested in attending, please RSVP HERE

ATLs
We no longer enter a "comment" for ATLs that are ""Seldom" or Inconsistent".  The new report card template does not have room for comments.  Please just enter the ATL rating of the following: 
- Seldom Meets Expectations
- Inconsistently Meets Expectations
- Consistency Expectations

Access to HS Office and Counseling Center Copy Room
We learned that teachers no longer have access to the HS Office and Counseling Center Copy Room after work hours.  This has now been rectified. Merry Christmas!

discussion.title: High School and the Master Facilities Plan
discussion.body:
Consider...
1)  Admissions, Head of School, Advancement relocating freeing up the 5th floor
2) On the eighth floor, removal of heating, AC units (equivalent space of +/- six classrooms.
In your groups, brainstorm ideas about how the existing space and additional space might be used.  What are the facilities priorities for the High School?
When you have your list, please discuss and establish the ten most important.  Then, please add the ten most important from your list to this Schoology discussion, in rank order (most important first).  Feel free to add more, but 10 most important is the minimum expectation.

discussion.title: The future of the High School
discussion.body:
Hi All,
When your group is ready and the discussions have finished, please post your notes and ideas on this discussion thread.
Please use the questions as heading to structure your answers.  Here are the questions again...
1) What should we be offering our students the chance to learn that we do not offer now?
 
2) What programs should we be considering adding/taking away to/from the High School experience?
 
3) In three years time (for example) what will further differentiate us in the HK school’s market as students and parents weigh their options?
